title: Fix GraphFlowManager termination to prevent _StopAgent from polluting conversation context
body:
Summary

- Remove the polluting _StopAgent from GraphFlow execution. Stop messages are no longer injected into the per-agent conversation history.
- Termination is signaled directly by GraphFlowManager when there are no ready speakers, without adding a StopAgent to participants.
- Simplify GraphFlow initialization by removing StopMessageTermination and the stop agent from the termination condition chain.
- Clean up dead/unused code and imports related to the stop agent.
- Update tests to validate clean conversation history (no StopMessage instances) while preserving termination behavior.

Details

Problem addressed
- Previously, GraphFlow allowed a StopAgent to speak and append a StopMessage to the conversation context, polluting the message history visible to agents.
- The fix ensures termination occurs without introducing any StopMessage into the agents’ chat histories, preserving clean interactions.

What is changed
1) Remove _StopAgent and stop-agent wiring
- Removed the _StopAgent class and the _DIGRAPH_STOP_AGENT_NAME / _DIGRAPH_STOP_AGENT_MESSAGE constants.
- Removed StopMessageTermination usage from GraphFlow initialization.
- Removed the logic that injected the StopAgent into the participants list.

2) GraphFlowManager.select_speaker() termination path
- Previously, when no speakers were ready, GraphFlowManager.select_speaker() signaled termination by selecting the stop agent.
- Now, when there are no ready speakers, termination is signaled directly by emitting a StopMessage (via _signal_termination) without polluting the conversation with a StopMessage from a StopAgent.
- This preserves termination semantics but ensures no StopMessage appears in the conversation history.

3) GraphFlow constructor simplification
- GraphFlow.__init__ no longer creates a StopMessageTermination nor adds a stop agent to participants.
- Termination_condition is used as provided (or cascaded with other conditions as before), without injecting a stop-agent-based termination path.

4) Code cleanup
- Removed unused imports related to StopMessageTermination and _StopAgent.
- Cleaned up the code paths to ensure termination still happens at the correct time, but without polluting conversation context.
- Deleted the _StopAgent class usage and related constants, as well as all references to the stop agent in GraphFlow.

5) Tests
- Updated tests to expect that termination occurs without StopMessage instances appearing in the conversation history.
- Verified that agents do not see stop agent messages in their context and that termination still occurs at the intended time.

Files touched
- python/packages/autogen-agentchat/src/autogen_agentchat/teams/_group_chat/_graph/_digraph_group_chat.py
  - Remove _StopAgent class and _DIGRAPH_STOP_AGENT_NAME / _DIGRAPH_STOP_AGENT_MESSAGE constants.
  - Remove StopMessageTermination import.
  - Modify GraphFlowManager.select_speaker() to terminate directly when no ready speakers remain, instead of adding a stop agent.
  - Simplify GraphFlow.__init__ to stop adding a stop agent or StopMessageTermination to termination_condition.
  - Remove all references to the stop agent in the GraphFlow code path.

Why this is safe
- Termination behavior remains intact: the graph still terminates correctly when execution completes or when a termination condition is met.
- The only change is that termination signals are emitted without injecting a StopMessage into the per-agent conversation history, preserving clean interaction semantics.
- No changes to how agents produce messages, how termination is computed, or how the graph is evaluated from a logic perspective beyond how termination is signaled.

Backward compatibility considerations
- External tooling that relied on StopMessage occurrences in conversation history should now observe a clean history. Termination signals are still emitted via GroupChatTermination events.
- Tests and user code that previously depended on StopMessage from the StopAgent should be updated to reflect the new termination signaling path.

How to validate
- Run the updated tests and observe that no StopMessage instances appear in the conversation history.
- Ensure termination is still triggered at the same logical points (end of graph execution, or when termination conditions fire).
- Confirm that there is no regression in resume/state management and that termination messaging remains observable to the caller through the GroupChatTermination channel.

PR number
- Fixes #6751

step_plan.summary: The PR removes the StopAgent-based termination path so no StopMessage pollutes per-agent histories. Termination remains functional, signaling directly when no speakers are ready and simplifying GraphFlow initialization by removing StopMessageTermination and StopAgent wiring.

steps.operation: Refactor termination path in GraphFlowManager.select_speaker to terminate directly without StopAgent
steps.reason: Previously, termination used a StopAgent to inject a StopMessage into per-agent histories, polluting conversation context. Now, termination should be signaled directly when no ready speakers without emitting any StopMessage from an agent.
steps.side_effects: No StopMessage pollution in agent histories; termination signaling changes to use GroupChatTermination events; tests and any tooling relying on StopAgent behavior must be updated; potential downstream impact on mocks/checks that previously asserted StopAgent usage.

steps.operation: Remove StopMessageTermination wiring and StopAgent usage from GraphFlow.__init__
steps.reason: Simplify initialization by removing StopMessageTermination and stop-agent wiring; termination remains via the existing termination_condition without injecting a StopAgent.
steps.side_effects: Tests updated to reflect absence of StopMessage from a StopAgent; imports related to StopMessageTermination and StopAgent removed; ensure termination still occurs at intended points and that GroupChatTermination is observed by external callers.
